Idiomatic Expressions

  • 里乘子曰 - A phrase indicating the author or narrator is speaking, similar to 'it is said' or 'the author says.'
  • 公直南书房时 - Refers to the official's time serving in the Nanxuefeng (Southern Study), a prestigious position in the Qing imperial court.
  • 文庙见所着貂服甚敝 - Describes the emperor seeing the official's fur robe, which is very worn out. '文庙' here likely refers to the emperor's presence in the palace.
  • 臣自授编修，仅此一袭，服之已二十馀年矣 - The official explains that he has only one fur robe, which he has worn for over twenty years since being appointed as a compiler (编修).
  • 上微笑颔之 - The emperor smiles and nods in acknowledgment.
  • 寻赐貂服，并拜典试之命 - Soon after, the emperor bestows a new fur robe upon him and appoints him as the chief examiner (典试).
  • 及任满入觐 - When his term is up, he comes to pay homage to the emperor.
  • 上问滇省变乱原委甚详 - The emperor asks in great detail about the causes and consequences of the unrest in Yunnan province.
  • 汝按临几郡 - The emperor asks how many prefectures he has inspected.
  • 以道梗均未按试 - The official responds that due to road blockages, he was unable to conduct examinations in all the prefectures.
  • 苦煞汝矣 - The emperor expresses sympathy, saying, 'You must have suffered greatly.'
  • 翌日，命兼摄顺天府丞事 - The next day, the emperor appoints him as the acting vice-governor (顺天府丞) of Shuntian Prefecture.
  • 谢恩时，上笑曰 - When the official thanks the emperor, the emperor laughs and says...
  • 顺天府丞，每届考期，卷资例有千金，聊偿在滇之苦可矣 - The emperor jokes that the vice-governorship provides a substantial income during exam periods, which can compensate for the hardships in Yunnan.
  • 其为文庙优眷如此 - The official frequently recounts how the emperor treated him with such favor.
  • 公每述及先朝高厚之恩，未尝不泣数行下也 - Whenever he recalls the emperor's kindness, the official cannot help but cry.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 庚申秋 - Specific year and season in the Chinese calendar, setting the temporal context.
  • 外夷至京师 - Literally 'foreign barbarians arrived at the capital,' referring to foreign invaders or enemies.
  • 乘舆北幸 - Euphemism for the emperor fleeing north due to an invasion or crisis.
  • 都城汹汹 - Describes the capital city in a state of panic or turmoil.
  • 大小臣工纷纷携眷迁避 - Refers to officials of all ranks and their families fleeing the city.
  • 各道胡同曲突绝烟 - Describes the city's streets and alleys being abandoned, with no smoke rising from chimneys.
  • 公时抱疴 - Indicates the subject was ill at the time.
  • 闻难痛哭 - Describes the subject's reaction to the crisis, crying out of grief.
  • 谓所亲陆眉生给谏日 - Formal way of saying 'told his close friend Lu Meisheng, who was an advisor.'
  • 子有老母，其行也乎哉 - Rhetorical question asking whether the friend should flee given that he has an elderly mother.
  • 宁慎持之勿妄动 - Advises caution and not to act rashly.
  • 免悔噬脐 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'to avoid regretting one's actions later.'
  • 陆慷慨誓死勿去 - Describes Lu Meisheng's resolve to stay despite the danger.
  • 公壮之 - Indicates the subject praised or admired Lu Meisheng's resolve.
  • 事定后，既出复入者，见公咸有愧色焉 - Describes how those who fled and returned later felt ashamed when they saw the subject.
  • 江阴何廉昉观察拭 - Refers to a person from Jiangyin named He Lianfang, who was an observer or inspector.
  • 侨居维扬 - Indicates that He Lianfang lived in Weiyang (present-day Yangzhou).
  • 筑壶园，座客常满 - Describes He Lianfang building a garden called Hutu Yuan, which was often full of guests.
  • 予尝于壶园座上得识诸公子 - Indicates that the author met the subject's sons at a gathering in Hutu Yuan.
  • 公次子礼园郎中宝清 - Refers to the subject's second son, Baohuaqing, who was a medical officer.
  • 博览群书，尤肆力于诗古文词 - Describes Baohuaqing as well-read and especially dedicated to poetry and classical prose.
  • 三子农上茂才宝让 - Refers to the subject's third son, Baorang, who was a talented scholar.
  • 何之聓也 - Indicates that Baorang was He Lianfang's son-in-law.
  • 皆能世公之学 - Means that both sons could carry on the subject's scholarly tradition.
  • 予幸与纪群交稔 - Indicates that the author was fortunate to have a close relationship with a group of scholars.
  • 知公生平大略 - Means that the author knew the subject's general life story.
  • 爰著于编 - Indicates that the author wrote about the subject in a compilation.
  • 藉以风世 - Means to use this as an example to inspire or influence the world.
  • 虽无老成人，尚有典刑 - Quote from the *Book of Songs* meaning 'Even though there are no old sages, there are still examples to follow.'
  • 惟公有焉 - Indicates that the subject was one such example.

  • 急令人舁伯 - Phrase meaning 'immediately order someone to carry the elder brother.' The verb 舁 (yú) is an archaic term for carrying, often used in the context of moving a corpse.
  • 自抱兒從與俱歸 - Phrase meaning 'carry the child himself and return together.' The verb 抱 (bào) means 'to carry' or 'to hold,' and 從與俱歸 (cóng yǔ jù guī) means 'to return together with them.'
  • 居然各慶再生矣 - Phrase meaning 'and indeed, they all celebrate as if reborn.' 居然 (jū rán) means 'indeed' or 'unexpectedly,' 各慶 (gè qìng) means 'each celebrates,' and 再生 (zài shēng) means 'reborn' or 'revived.'
  • 先是，仲以伯既死 - Phrase meaning 'before this, the second brother, because the elder brother had died.' 先是 (xiān shì) means 'before this,' 仲 (zhòng) refers to the second brother, 以 (yǐ) means 'because,' and 既死 (jì sǐ) means 'had died.'
  • 慮生兒不能并其產 - Phrase meaning 'was concerned that the living child could not inherit his property.' 處 (lǜ) means 'to be concerned,' 生兒 (shēng ér) means 'living child,' 不能并其產 (bù néng bìng qí chǎn) means 'could not inherit his property.'
  • 以某妪素为人接生 - Phrase meaning 'because a certain old woman has always been a midwife.' 以 (yǐ) means 'because,' 某妪 (mǒu yù) means 'a certain old woman,' 素 (sù) means 'always' or 'usually,' 为人接生 (wéi rén jiē shēng) means 'to be a midwife.'
  • 重贿之曰 - Phrase meaning 'he heavily bribed her, saying.' 重贿 (zhòng huì) means 'heavily bribed,' 之 (zhī) is a pronoun meaning 'her,' and 曰 (yuē) means 'said.'
  • 女也则已 - Phrase meaning 'if it is a girl, then it is enough.' 女也 (nǚ yě) means 'if it is a girl,' 则已 (zé yǐ) means 'then it is enough.'
  • 男也则为计戕之 - Phrase meaning 'if it is a boy, then plan to kill it.' 男也 (nán yě) means 'if it is a boy,' 则为计戕之 (zé wéi jì qiāng zhī) means 'then plan to kill it.'
  • 愿以五十金为寿 - Phrase meaning 'I wish to give fifty gold pieces as a gift.' 愿 (yuàn) means 'I wish,' 以 (yǐ) means 'to give,' 五十金 (wǔ shí jīn) means 'fifty gold pieces,' 为寿 (wèi shòu) means 'as a gift.'
  • 妪初不肯 - Phrase meaning 'the old woman initially refused.' 媪 (yù) means 'old woman,' 初 (chū) means 'initially,' 不肯 (bù kěn) means 'refused.'
  • 请倍之 - Phrase meaning 'asked for double the amount.' 请 (qǐng) means 'asked,' 倍之 (bèi zhī) means 'double the amount.'
  • 妪利其贿 - Phrase meaning 'the old woman was lured by the bribe.' 利 (lì) means 'lured' or 'benefited,' 其贿 (qí huì) means 'the bribe.'
  • 乃诺之 - Phrase meaning 'and agreed to it.' 乃 (nǎi) means 'and,' 诺之 (nuò zhī) means 'agreed to it.'
  • 为嫂接生时 - Phrase meaning 'when the sister-in-law was giving birth.' 为嫂 (wéi sǎo) means 'sister-in-law,' 接生 (jiē shēng) means 'giving birth,' 时 (shí) means 'when.'
  • 悄以花针纳儿脐中 - Phrase meaning 'secretly inserted a needle into the child's navel.' 悄 (qiǎo) means 'secretly,' 以 (yǐ) means 'with,' 花针 (huā zhēn) means 'needle,' 纳儿脐中 (nà ér qí zhōng) means 'inserted into the child's navel.'
  • 而他人不知也 - Phrase meaning 'but others did not know about it.' 而 (ér) means 'but,' 他人 (tā rén) means 'others,' 不知也 (bù zhī yě) means 'did not know about it.'
  • 至是仲与妪被击 - Phrase meaning 'at this point, the second brother and the old woman were struck.' 至是 (zhì shì) means 'at this point,' 仲与妪 (zhòng yǔ yù) means 'the second brother and the old woman,' 被击 (bèi jī) means 'were struck.'
  • 各手持元宝一枚 - Phrase meaning 'each held one ingot of silver.' 各 (gè) means 'each,' 手 (shǒu) means 'hand,' 持 (chí) means 'held,' 元宝 (yuán bǎo) means 'ingot of silver,' 一枚 (yī méi) means 'one.'
  • 脐针则拔插妪额 - Phrase meaning 'the needle in the navel was pulled out and stuck in the old woman's forehead.' 脐针 (qí zhēn) means 'needle in the navel,' 则 (zé) means 'then,' 拔插 (bá chā) means 'pulled out and stuck,' 媪额 (yù é) means 'old woman's forehead.'
  • 其半入骨 - Phrase meaning 'half of it went into the bone.' 其半 (qí bàn) means 'half of it,' 入骨 (rù gǔ) means 'went into the bone.'
  • 血缕缕犹未绝也 - Phrase meaning 'the blood was still flowing.' 血缕缕 (xuè lǚ lǚ) means 'blood was still flowing,' 犹 (yóu) means 'still,' 未绝 (wèi jué) means 'not yet stopped.'
  • 噫！谁谓彼苍者愦愦哉！ - Phrase meaning 'Ah! Who would say that the heavens are so foolish!' 噫 (yī) is an exclamation, 谁谓 (shuí wèi) means 'who would say,' 彼苍者 (bǐ cāng zhě) means 'the heavens,' 愦愦 (kuì kuì) means 'foolish,' 哉 (zāi) is an exclamation.
  • 众议以伯与儿棺殓仲、妪 - Phrase meaning 'the crowd decided to bury the elder brother and the child in the same coffin with the second brother and the old woman.' 众议 (zhòng yì) means 'the crowd decided,' 以 (yǐ) means 'to,' 伯与儿 (bó yǔ ér) means 'the elder brother and the child,' 棺殓 (guān liàn) means 'to bury in a coffin,' 仲、妪 (zhòng, yù) means 'the second brother and the old woman.'
  • 即同葬其穴 - Phrase meaning 'and bury them together in the same grave.' 即 (jí) means 'and,' 同葬 (tóng zàng) means 'bury together,' 其穴 (qí xué) means 'in the same grave.'
  • 后伯夫妇寿至八十 - Phrase meaning 'later, the elder brother and his wife lived to be eighty.' 后 (hòu) means 'later,' 伯夫妇 (bó fū fù) means 'the elder brother and his wife,' 寿至 (shòu zhì) means 'lived to be,' 八十 (bā shí) means 'eighty.'
  • 生四子 - Phrase meaning 'had four sons.' 生 (shēng) means 'had,' 四子 (sì zǐ) means 'four sons.'
  • 怜仲无子，以一嗣焉 - Phrase meaning 'pitied the second brother for having no son, and adopted one.' 怜 (lián) means 'pitied,' 仲 (zhòng) refers to the second brother, 无子 (wú zǐ) means 'having no son,' 以一嗣焉 (yǐ yī sì yān) means 'adopted one.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 或謂雷擊仲、妪誠快，可惜太晚 - Colloquial expression of regret and satisfaction: 'Some say that striking Zhong and the old woman would indeed be satisfying, but it is too late.'
  • 倘擊於二人賄商時，不較直捷乎 - Conditional statement expressing a desire for immediate justice: 'If they were struck when they were bribing merchants, wouldn't it be more direct?'
  • 伯之再生，實嫂之烈感之也 - Poetic way of attributing the elder brother's survival to the wife's strong emotions: 'The elder brother's rebirth is truly due to the wife's passionate influence.'
  • 伯何由再生也耶 - Rhetorical question emphasizing the improbability of the elder brother's survival: 'How could the elder brother have been reborn?'
  • 伯所素知 - Suggests that the elder brother is aware of the wife's virtues: 'What the elder brother has always known.'
  • 天必試其果否 - Reflects a belief in divine testing of human virtue: 'Heaven must test whether they are truly virtuous.'
  • 迨兒死取決意絕食從伯地下 - Describes the wife's extreme act of devotion: 'When the child died, she decided to starve herself and follow the elder brother to the grave.'
  • 嚎啕干哭，人不忍聞 - Emphasizes the intensity of her grief: 'She wailed and cried so loudly that no one could bear to hear it.'
  • 天聽在上而忍聞之乎 - Suggests that Heaven is moved by her sorrow: 'How could Heaven above bear to hear it?'
  • 特於冬藏時為嫂破格奮其一擊之威 - Describes the timing and intensity of the divine intervention: 'Especially during the winter storage season, Heaven broke its usual pattern and struck with its full force for the sake of the wife.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 雷擊 - Refers to a lightning strike, often seen as a divine punishment in Chinese culture.
  • 仲、妪 - Refers to the elder brother and the old woman, respectively.
  • 伯 - Refers to the elder brother, a common term for an older sibling.
  • 嫂 - Refers to the elder brother's wife, a term for an older sister-in-law.
  • 冬藏時 - Refers to the winter storage season, a time when people traditionally store food and prepare for the colder months.

Proper Nouns

  • 里乘子 - A title or name, possibly referring to a scholar or writer.

  • 妪 - Refers to the old woman, possibly the elder brother's wife.
  • 伯 - Refers to the elder brother.
  • 嫂 - Refers to the elder brother's wife.
  • 冬藏時 - Refers to the winter storage season.

  • 掷儿水中 - Threw the child into the water; a shocking and morally ambiguous action.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 车子 - A cart driver, representing a low social status in classical Chinese society.
  • 白金 - Refers to silver, a valuable commodity in ancient China, often used as currency.
  • 螟蛉 - A term for an adopted child, reflecting the ancient Chinese practice of adoption.
  • 雷击车子死 - Lightning striking someone dead is a common motif in classical Chinese literature, often used to signify divine retribution.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 春夏之交 - Refers to the transitional period between spring and summer.
  • 苦早 - Literally 'suffer from drought,' indicating severe dry conditions.
  • 早禾无收 - Literally 'early rice has no harvest,' meaning the early rice crop failed due to the drought.
  • 甫得甘霖 - Literally 'just received sweet rain,' referring to a timely and much-needed rainfall.
  • 农人心稍慰 - Literally 'the farmers' hearts are somewhat comforted,' indicating a slight relief after the rain.
  • 佥谓 - A formal expression meaning 'all agreed' or 'it was generally believed.'
  • 种荞可以救荒 - Literally 'planting buckwheat can save from famine,' suggesting that buckwheat could be a solution to food shortages.
  • 苦无种 - Literally 'suffering from lack of seed,' indicating a shortage of buckwheat seeds.
  • 藏陈荞甚多 - Literally 'hid a lot of old buckwheat,' referring to the storage of a large quantity of old buckwheat.
  • 虑颗瘪色黯 - Literally 'worried about the seeds being shriveled and dark,' indicating concerns about the quality of the buckwheat seeds.
  • 以甑蒸之 - Literally 'steamed it with a steamer,' referring to the process of steaming the buckwheat.
  • 顿觉硕大光润 - Literally 'suddenly felt large and lustrous,' indicating that the steamed buckwheat appeared larger and shinier.
  • 出卖荞种 - Literally 'selling buckwheat seeds,' referring to the act of selling buckwheat seeds.
  • 荞价翔贵 - Literally 'the price of buckwheat soared,' indicating a significant increase in the price of buckwheat.
  • 获利千万 - Literally 'made a profit of millions,' indicating a substantial financial gain.
  • 所种荞竟无一出者 - Literally 'the planted buckwheat did not produce a single sprout,' indicating that the buckwheat failed to grow.
  • 众无所归 - Literally 'the people had nowhere to turn,' indicating that the people were left with no options.
  • 怨叹为天意使然 - Literally 'complained and lamented that it was the will of heaven,' indicating that the people attributed their misfortune to divine will.
  • 饥饿以死者指不胜屈 - Literally 'those who died of hunger could not be counted,' indicating a large number of deaths due to starvation.
  • 雷击某甲死 - Literally 'struck by lightning, Mr. A died,' indicating that Mr. A was struck by lightning and died.
  • 釜底书 - Literally 'wrote on the bottom of the pot,' referring to a message written on the bottom of a cooking pot.
  • 蒸荞误人，粉身莫赎 - Literally 'steaming buckwheat misled people, even if ground into powder, it cannot be redeemed,' indicating that the act of steaming buckwheat caused harm and could not be forgiven.
  • 险恶 - Literally 'cunning and evil,' indicating that Mr. A was both crafty and malevolent.
  • 天诛 - The concept of divine retribution, often used in Chinese literature to explain why certain individuals face severe consequences for their actions.
  • 国法 - The legal system and laws of the state, which were often seen as less severe than divine punishment.
  • 律例 - Specific legal statutes and regulations that were part of the legal system.
  • 情理 - The moral and ethical principles that guide human behavior, often considered more important than strict adherence to the law.
  • 经 - The traditional texts and teachings that form the basis of Chinese culture and philosophy.
  • 权 - The practical considerations and pragmatism that sometimes override strict adherence to the letter of the law.
  • 车子溺婴 - Literally 'Mr. Zhi drowned an infant,' indicating that Mr. Zhi drowned an infant.
  • 十三人以五十千钱而伤二命 - Literally 'thirteen people with fifty thousand coins injured two lives,' indicating that thirteen people with a significant amount of money caused harm to two lives.
  • 执律例 - Literally 'adhering to legal statutes,' referring to following the letter of the law.
  • 揆诸情理 - Literally 'judging by human sentiments and reason,' referring to considering moral and ethical implications.
  • 恶作剧 - Literally 'mischief,' referring to mischievous or harmful actions.
  • 贷之以生 - Literally 'granting them life,' indicating sparing someone's life.
  • 明明在上 - Literally 'the bright and clear one above,' referring to a higher power or divine authority.
  • 蚩蚩之氓 - Literally 'the foolish and ignorant masses,' referring to ordinary people who are not wise or informed.
  • 存警畏 - Literally 'to keep a sense of fear and caution,' indicating a need to remain vigilant and cautious.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 素性暴逆 - Describes someone with a naturally violent and rebellious disposition.
  • 奴畜之 - Treats someone as a slave or servant, indicating a lack of respect.
  • 烹鹿蹄失饪 - Refers to cooking deer hooves improperly, which is a specific culinary mistake.
  • 挞几死 - Means to beat someone almost to death.
  • 每以规劝 - Means to frequently advise or counsel.
  • 并遭箠楚 - Indicates that both the wife and the mother were beaten severely.
  • 网得生兔 - Means to catch a live rabbit with a net.
  • 绳弛逸去 - Describes how the rabbit escaped by breaking free from the rope.
  • 伏匿积薪中 - Means to hide among piles of firewood.
  • 拔剑向妻问母所在 - Means to draw a sword and ask the wife where the mother is.
  • 老畜得毋匿积薪中 - A rhetorical question asking if the mother might be hiding among the firewood.
  • 亟出狂奔 - Means to rush out and run wildly.
  • 往追 - Means to go and chase after.
  • 力揽其襟 - Describes the wife holding onto the husband's robe with all her strength.
  • 奋不得脱 - Means to struggle but fail to break free.
  • 益怒 - Means to become even more angry.
  • 杀妻，并断其手 - Means to kill the wife and cut off her hand.
  • 过关圣庙 - Refers to passing by a temple dedicated to Guan Yu, a revered general in Chinese history.
  • 大声哭且呼曰 - Means to cry out loudly while calling for help.
  • 关爷救命！关爷救命！ - A plea for help directed to Guan Yu, often referred to as 'Lord Guan.'
  • 刚至庙门 - Means to just reach the temple gate.
  • 圣帝座前泥塑周将军蓦驰出 - Describes a clay statue of Zhou General suddenly rushing out from in front of the sacred emperor's throne.
  • 以木刀腰斩某倒地上 - Means to cut the person in half with a wooden sword, causing them to fall to the ground.
  • 远近闻而来观 - Means that people from far and near came to see what happened.
  • 见神像一足立户外 - Describes seeing a statue with one foot outside the door.
  • 瞪目望尸 - Means to stare at the corpse with wide eyes.
  • 须眉奕奕 - Describes the statue's eyebrows and mustache as vivid and lifelike.
  • 若有馀怒 - Means to appear as if still angry.
  • 刀上血犹淋漓滴沥也 - Describes blood still dripping from the wooden sword.
  • 舁去某尸 - Means to carry away the body.
  • 拭净血迹 - Means to clean the bloodstains.
  • 醵资延道士礼蘸三日 - Means to raise money and invite a Taoist priest to perform rituals for three days.
  • 率其母刑牲祷祭 - Means to lead the mother to sacrifice animals and pray.
  • 妥神归庙 - Means to pacify the spirit and return it to the temple.
  • 自是不孝者闻之，皆悚然改辄 - Means that after this, those who were disrespectful to their parents became frightened and changed their ways.
  • 不敢再忤其亲矣 - Means they no longer dared to disobey their parents.
